response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically capable senior individual contributor with breadth across distributed systems, real-time infrastructure, and early-stage agentic tooling at Stripe, but the CTO role requires demonstrated executive leadership—scaling and managing engineering organizations, architectural ownership of multi-agent AI systems in production, and board-level communication—none of which appear anywhere in his career history. His AI/agent work is recent, contributory rather than architectural, and his entire career has been spent as an IC; there is no evidence of managing a team, let alone scaling one from 10 to 30+ people.
strengths: Solid distributed-systems and high-throughput backend experience (Dunnhumby microservices handling millions of req/s, real-time low-latency systems with async I/O)
Active exposure to agentic frameworks and MCP tooling at Stripe, demonstrating current awareness of the AI-agent infrastructure space
Meaningful fintech adjacency through roles at RIDE Capital and Stripe, including some familiarity with payment and investment infrastructure
concerns: Zero people-management or engineering-leadership experience across 17+ years of employment—every role is an individual contributor title with no reported reports, hiring, or org design
Multi-agent AI experience is superficial and very recent (April 2025), limited to contributing to existing frameworks rather than owning architecture or operating systems with financial consequences
No evidence of executive responsibilities: no board communication, no budgeting, no outsourced-team governance, no technical strategy ownership at any scope
